front: Fanfare: opens with solemn percussion — timpani, bass drum, and tam-tam — for approximately five bars before any brass enter
Fanfare: three trumpets enter in unison on ascending notes F, B-flat, F as the heroic main theme
Fanfare: scored exclusively for brass (four French horns, three trumpets, three trombones) and percussion — no strings or woodwinds
Fanfare: commissioned by Eugene Goossens for the Cincinnati Symphony Orchestra in 1942 as one of 18 wartime fanfares
Fanfare: inspired by Vice President Henry Wallace's speech 'The Price of Free World Victory,' which proclaimed the 'Century of the Common Man'
Fanfare: the main theme was later incorporated into the finale of this composer's Third Symphony (1946)
Fanfare: culminates in a D major chord; Emerson, Lake and Palmer recorded a famous cover — the composer preferred his own version
Composer: used the Shaker hymn 'Simple Gifts' in a ballet choreographed by Martha Graham depicting pioneers building a farmhouse in Pennsylvania
Composer: studied harmony with Nadia Boulanger at Fontainebleau
Composer: Clarinet Concerto commissioned by Benny Goodman; two movements linked by a cadenza for the solo instrument
Work: narrated piece that quotes 'Camptown Races' and the Gettysburg Address; commissioned by Andre Kostelanetz
Ballet: includes sections 'Hoe-Down' and 'Saturday Night Waltz'
Composer: described three 'planes' of listening — sensuous, expressive, and sheerly musical — in a book on how to listen to music
back: Aaron Copland (What to Listen for in Music)

front: Composer: wrote Quiet City for trumpet, English horn, and string orchestra, based on incidental music for an Irwin Shaw play depicting a man haunted by a trumpet
Composer: late in career embraced twelve-tone composition in Piano Fantasy and Connotations
Fanfare: written in direct response to Copland's fanfare, using the same brass-and-percussion instrumentation; dedicated to conductor Marin Alsop
back: Fanfare for the Uncommon Woman (Joan Tower)
work: Joan Tower – Fanfare for the Uncommon Woman

front: Conductor: dedicatee of Joan Tower's Fanfare for the Uncommon Woman; became the first woman to direct the Last Night of the Proms in 2013
work: Joan Tower – Fanfare for the Uncommon Woman

front: Composer: co-founded the Da Capo Chamber Players; won three Grammys for Made in America (2008); known for a series of six fanfares responding to a Copland work
work: Joan Tower – Fanfare for the Uncommon Woman
